stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model.
It is categorized under key ML/AI topics such as 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The context suggests this is a benchmark/resource listing rather than a detailed breakthrough announcement.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, llama, and AI/ML communities.

stories.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for instant voice-to-voice translation
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for real-time voice-to-voice translation.
The new feature preserves nuanced vocal qualities like tone, pacing, and pitch.
Security is enhanced through the implementation of SynthID watermarks.

stories.title: ABC-Bench: An Agentic Bio-Capabilities Benchmark for Biosecurity
stories.author: Seth Donoughe
bullet_points: LLMs are gaining significant capabilities in biological research, including data interpretation and in silico biology tasks.
The rise of these AI capabilities presents both opportunities for scientific discovery and new biosecurity risks.
The paper introduces 'ABC-Bench,' a benchmark designed to evaluate the agentic bio-capabilities of LLMs.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The entry highlights the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is tagged with relevant technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on text-generation capabilities.
The model utilizes key ML technologies including transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, offering faster local AI inference on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy.
The core value proposition is running AI capabilities entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: BYD to install thousands of 5-minute EV chargers across Europe
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: BYD plans to deploy 3,000 superfast Flash Chargers across Europe by the end of next year.
This expansion is part of BYD's strategy to accelerate its market penetration in Europe.
Initial installations have already begun in key markets, including Germany and the UK.

stories.title: Commonwealth Fusion makes the physics case for its 400 MW reactor
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Commonwealth Fusion is advancing its design and modeling for a 400 MW reactor.
The progress is substantiated by five new peer-reviewed scientific papers.
This represents a significant step in the development of advanced fusion energy technology.

stories.title: Anthropic says these topics are too dangerous to let its Fable 5 model talk about
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has implemented safety guardrails on its Fable 5 model.
The model is explicitly restricted from discussing topics deemed too dangerous.
Prohibited subjects include cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry queries.

stories.title: NASA assigns crew for Artemis III, sets aggressive timeline for flying it
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has officially assigned the crew for the Artemis III mission.
The mission is characterized as an 'extraordinary demonstration of what is possible.'
The announcement sets an aggressive timeline for the execution of Artemis III.

stories.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Introduces a 'Data Journalist Agent' designed to automate the end-to-end process of transforming raw data into trustworthy, multimodal stories.
Addresses the current limitation where existing AI agents handle only isolated steps (e.g., data analysis or visual design) rather than the holistic journalistic workflow.
The research aims to bridge the gap between raw data and accessible, high-quality news features that require complex contextualization and narrative structuring.

stories.title: PhantomBench: Benchmarking the Non-existential Threat of Language Models
stories.author: Hila Gonen
bullet_points: Introduces PhantomBench, a new large-scale benchmark specifically designed to test language model hallucinations.
Addresses the critical risk of LMs generating factually ungrounded responses, especially in high-stakes applications.
Focuses on evaluating how reliably LMs can recognize the boundaries and limits of their own knowledge.

stories.title: The Shibboleth Effect: Auditing the Cross-Lingual Distributional Skew of Large Language Models
stories.author: Hakan Mehmetcik
bullet_points: Investigates the 'Shibboleth Effect'—cross-lingual distributional skew—in frontier LLMs under adversarial stress.
Uses a complex, synthetic geopolitical wargame ('Cerulean Sea Crisis') to test model behavior in conflict scenarios.
Compares six leading LLMs (including GPT-4o, Llama-4, Gemini-3.1-Pro) to assess cross-lingual robustness.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: Valve is phasing out physical Steam gift cards due to scammers
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Valve is discontinuing the sale of physical Steam gift cards.
The decision is attributed to the ongoing impact of scammers on Steam users.
Steam will no longer restock physical gift cards once their current inventory is depleted.

stories.title: Racist comments targeting politicians tripled since Meta relaxed its rules
stories.author: David Gilbert, wired.com
bullet_points: Racist comments targeting politicians have reportedly tripled since Meta adjusted its content moderation policies.
The surge in harmful content is accompanied by an increase in violent threats directed at lawmakers on Facebook.
The story highlights the complex relationship between platform policy changes and the spread of online hate and threats.

stories.title: GM Energy introduces V2G support and new energy storage battery chemistry
stories.author: Roberto Baldwin
bullet_points: GM Energy is expanding its EV capabilities by introducing Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) support.
The company is also rolling out new energy storage battery chemistry for its vehicles.
A significant portion of GM's EV fleet (over 250,000 units) is already equipped with V2G capability.

stories.title: Starlink charges $10 monthly hardware fee in move away from one-time purchases
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Starlink is implementing a new pricing structure, shifting from one-time hardware purchases to a monthly hardware fee.
The service subscription price has also been increased by $5, raising the total to $10.
This change impacts Starlink, SpaceX's primary revenue stream, signaling a shift in their business model.

stories.title: Locked in heated rivalry with researcher, Microsoft fixes 0-day they disclosed
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has addressed a zero-day vulnerability that was disclosed by a researcher.
The news highlights a competitive dynamic between the researcher and Microsoft.
Another zero-day vulnerability, also disclosed by Nightmare Eclipse, appears to have been patched.

stories.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
stories.author: Cho-Jui Hsieh
bullet_points: The paper reinterprets Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) not just as maximizing token likelihood, but as a problem of target distribution design.
It argues that strictly fitting to observed tokens can be suboptimal due to noise, non-uniqueness, or misalignment with the model's prior knowledge.
The research shifts focus from the loss objective to analyzing the token-level target that the training process drives.

stories.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
stories.author: Mengdi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EEVEE, a novel framework for test-time prompt learning in LLM agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current methods by supporting multi-dataset, real-world task streams.
Employs a router mechanism to mitigate cross-dataset interference during learning.

stories.title: Piper: A Programmable Distributed Training System
stories.author: Stephanie Wang
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Piper,' a programmable distributed training system designed for large-scale model training.
It addresses the current bottleneck where manual design of complex, multi-strategy parallelism (data, pipeline, expert) is required for foundation model pretraining.
Piper aims to automate or simplify the integration of various parallelism strategies and memory optimizations (like ZeRO) into training workflows.

stories.title: Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative
stories.author: Marc Scott
bullet_points: The narrative that LLMs match human experts in knowledge tasks is being questioned.
Current benchmarks often measure performance on data already present in the LLM's training set.
A key limitation is the lack of assessment regarding the reliability or magnitude of LLM performance.

stories.title: ReasonAlloc: Hierarchical Decoding-Time KV Cache Budget Allocation for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Shuang Qiu
bullet_points: Addresses inference bottlenecks in LLM reasoning caused by rapid Key-Value (KV) cache growth during long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trajectories.
Proposes 'ReasonAlloc,' a method for hierarchical, decoding-time KV cache budget allocation.
Overcomes limitations of current methods by moving beyond uniform budget distribution and addressing dynamic context demands during inference.

stories.title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
stories.author: Randall Balestriero
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of systematic understanding regarding when Cross-modal Alignment (CA) versus Cross-modal Prediction (CP) is effective in multimodal learning.
It proposes a 'Phase Diagram' to help practitioners diagnose when standard multimodal training methods succeed or fail.
The research is highly relevant for scientific domains (e.g., biomedicine, astrophysics) dealing with heterogeneous data and multiple measurement levels.

stories.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
stories.author: Cho-Jui Hsieh
bullet_points: The paper reinterprets Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) from a simple likelihood maximization to a problem of target distribution design.
It argues that strictly fitting to observed tokens in SFT can be suboptimal due to noise, non-uniqueness, or misalignment with the model's prior knowledge.
The research analyzes the token-level target that the standard SFT loss objective implicitly drives the model toward.

stories.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
stories.author: Mengdi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EEVEE, a novel framework for test-time prompt learning in LLM agents.
Addresses a critical gap in current methods by supporting multi-dataset learning for real-world, heterogeneous task streams.
Utilizes a router mechanism within EEVEE to mitigate cross-dataset interference during adaptation.

stories.title: Predicting Future Behaviors in Reasoning Models Enables Better Steering
stories.author: Wojciech Samek
bullet_points: The paper addresses the issue of unpredictable behavior in large reasoning models (LRMs) during deployment.
Existing 'test-time steering' methods, which intervene on hidden representations, can degrade output quality.
The research proposes using activation probes trained to predict *future* behavior as a more effective intervention target than current detection features.

stories.title: COGENT: Continuous Graph Emulators with Neural Ordinary Differential Equations for Long-Term Physical Forecasting
stories.author: Maryam Rahnemoonfar
bullet_points: Introduces COGENT, a new continuous graph emulator designed for long-term physical forecasting.
It utilizes Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODEs) to model the temporal evolution of systems.
The model processes irregular geospatial meshes by encoding system states and forcing fields using a graph-based history encoder.

stories.title: Itô maps for any-step SDEs
stories.author: Jakiw Pidstrigach
bullet_points: Introduces the 'Itô map,' a novel framework for modeling any-step stochastic differential equations (SDEs).
Addresses a gap in current generative models by providing a single-pass prediction mechanism for stochastic dynamics, unlike ODE-based methods.
The formulation offers new estimators applicable to inference-time control problems in stochastic systems.

stories.title: OncoTraj: a public benchmark for longitudinal resistance prediction in EGFR-mutant non-small-cell lung cancer on osimertinib
stories.author: Aarchi Singh Thakur
bullet_points: Introduces OncoTraj, a new public benchmark dataset for studying resistance evolution in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
The benchmark specifically focuses on predicting longitudinal resistance to the drug osimertinib.
The dataset is derived from 813 real-world clinical-genomic patient trajectories, addressing a gap in computational modeling resources.

stories.title: Modeling Complex Behaviors: Multi-Personality Composition and Dynamic Switching in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Zhou Su
bullet_points: The research addresses the need to control complex behaviors in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for social interaction.
It introduces explicit personality conditioning and a systematic evaluation framework for single and multi-personality induction.
Experiments indicate that while personality induction improves image captioning, it can negatively impact performance.

stories.title: Mercedes‑Benz starts large‑scale production of electric axial flux motor
stories.author: raffael_de
bullet_points: Mercedes-Benz is initiating mass production of electric axial flux motors.
This signals a significant step in the automotive industry's transition to electric vehicles (EVs).
Axial flux motors represent an advanced motor technology being adopted by major manufacturers.
